characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Cool Old Guy: Ben is a man past his prime with hair already grayed out, but he remains a good shot with the revolver and is still charmingly charismatic. Good Parents: Ben single-handedly raised his three sons to become good and honorable men of valor by instilling into them the same principles that helped shape him into what he is. Non-Idle Rich: The owner of the biggest spread of the Nevada Territory. He’s engaged in physical labor on his own ranch and is very active in his community. Papa Wolf: If you want to make Ben get real angry real fast then threaten one of his sons. He also does not appreciate any children, in general, being threatened. Someone to Remember Her By: In a rare male example of this trope, Ben has one son from each of his three dead wives: Adam from Elizabeth Stoddard, Hoss (Eric) from Inger Borgstrom and Little Joe from Marie De Marigny.

characters.name: Ben Cartwright
characters.description: Cool Old Guy: Ben is a man past his prime with hair already grayed out, but he remains a good shot with the revolver and is still charmingly charismatic. Good Parents: Ben single-handedly raised his three sons to become good and honorable men of valor by instilling into them the same principles that helped shape him into what he is. Non-Idle Rich: The owner of the biggest spread of the Nevada Territory. He’s engaged in physical labor on his own ranch and is very active in his community. Papa Wolf: If you want to make Ben get real angry real fast then threaten one of his sons. He also does not appreciate any children, in general, being threatened. Someone to Remember Her By: In a rare male example of this trope, Ben has one son from each of his three dead wives: Adam from Elizabeth Stoddard, Hoss (Eric) from Inger Borgstrom and Little Joe from Marie De Marigny.

characters.name: Adam Cartwright
characters.description: Ambiguously Gay: Unlike the rest of the Cartwright clan, he's less than eager to get married, being single in his thirties at a time when that was rare for a man of his social standing. Many of the times hedoesromance a woman He finally proposes to the widow Laura Dayton in Season 5, but his hesitation to set a wedding date gives his cousin Will time to woo Laura himself. When he discovers them, Adam encourages them to get married, admitting that he was more in love with theideaof having his own family than with Laura as a woman. He also has a habit of quickly becoming close friends with men who his family or the townsfolk are convinced are bad news, and forcefully defending their innocence in spite of any and all evidence to the contrary. The Ace: Educated, witty, handsome, musically talented, a good businessman, and more than able to hold his own with a gun or his fists, he's considered one of the most eligible bachelors in the Nevada Territory.Hoss: Dadburnit, seem like everything old Adam turn his hand to just turns out smooth and perfect. Never a hitch. Yes, sir.Just seems like Adam ain't got no problems ever, or troubles.Broken Ace: A lifetime of being put on a pedestal by his family and the brutality of life in the Wild West have taken their toll on his psyche. Broken Ace: A lifetime of being put on a pedestal by his family and the brutality of life in the Wild West have taken their toll on his psyche. Big Brother Instinct: Adam does not appreciate violence or threats against Hoss or Little Joe. He also defends them (or at least tries to) fromwomen who would take advantage of them. Deadpan Snarker: Quick with a sardonic quip as he is with a gun.

characters.name: Hoss Cartwright
characters.description: Beware the Nice Ones: Hoss is one of the friendliest guys you'll ever meet... as long as you don't try to harm anyone he cares about. Big Eater: ARunning Gag. Gentle Giant: Hoss is one of the biggest men in Nevada territory. He is also one of the kindest. Only Known by Their Nickname: No one calls him Eric. It’slampshadedat his birth.Ben: We'll give him both names and see which one sticks.

characters.name: Little Joe Cartwright
characters.description: The Baby of the Bunch: Before Jamie joins the group in Season 12, he is this, being only seventeen at the start of the first season. The Casanova: Like father, like son. Hair-Trigger Temper: It doesn't take much to get him riled up. Pretty Boy: He is played by a young Michael Landon after all.

characters.name: Hop Sing
characters.description: Supreme Chef: He is one, as everyone who stays for dinner at the Cartwrights' can attest. Hoss, in particular, is a big fan.
